archetype: Real-Time Isotope-Dilution Mass Spectrometry (IDMS) Calibration & Matrix Interference Correction Engine for High-Throughput Clinical Toxicology
domain: Forensic Clinical Toxicology & Mass Spectrometry Instrumentation
description: A real-time control plane that orchestrates liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) workflows to dynamically adjust isotope-labeled internal standard ratios and correct for matrix effects (ion suppression/enhancement) during live sample analysis. Used by forensic toxicologists and instrument operators, it processes high-frequency ion current telemetry to prevent false negatives in drug-facilitated crime investigations. The system is technically arduous due to the need for sub-second latency in adjusting micro-valve pumps and electrometer gains based on non-linear ionization physics in complex biological matrices.
rules: MUST ingest and correlate raw ion current telemetry via the Thermo Fisher TSQ or Sciex QTRAP proprietary binary stream protocols with <10ms latency.
MUST enforce strict Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) 21 CFR Part 11 audit trails for every single automated parameter adjustment made during a run.
MUST implement real-time signal processing algorithms (e.g., Savitzky-Golay filtering and baseline correction) to distinguish isobaric interference from target analytes.
MUST dynamically recalibrate the 'matrix effect factor' for each specific patient sample based on the internal standard recovery ratio before releasing quantitation results.
MUST support the integration of external Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS) using ASTM E1394 or HL7 v2.x standards for bidirectional result verification.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use batch-processing or ETL pipelines for data analysis, as the system must make real-time hardware control decisions (valve switching) while the chromatogram is still running to prevent sample carryover.

DO NOT rely on generic SQL transactions for time-series ion current data, as the write throughput (20kHz+ per detector) and append-only nature of chromatographic data require specialized time-series or columnar storage.

DO NOT implement 'human-in-the-loop' confirmation for every matrix correction event, as this would halt the high-throughput pipeline; instead, use pre-validated algorithmic confidence intervals with post-run exception reporting.
key_technical_challenges: Synchronization of sub-millisecond hardware control signals (mass analyzer voltage ramps) with high-frequency (20kHz) detector data streams to ensure temporal alignment during ion suppression events.
Real-time execution of non-linear regression models to correct for co-eluting matrix interference without exceeding the processing window between chromatographic peaks.
Ensuring deterministic failure handling where a single dropped packet in the telemetry stream does not result in data corruption that invalidates a forensic legal chain of custody.

recommended_stack.language: Rust + C FFI bindings (for memory safety and direct low-level access to instrument DSPs without GC pauses)
recommended_stack.framework: Actix-web with raw UDP/TCP stream handling (for high-throughput, low-latency telemetry ingestion)
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB (for efficient compression and time-bucketed querying of continuous ion current streams)
recommended_stack.cache: Redis Cluster (for ephemeral state of active injection sequences and fast-access calibration curves)
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka (for decoupling the high-volume instrument stream from the LIMS reporting pipeline)
